steps.title: Use the sample size effect
steps.content: For a random sample, a larger sample size usually gives a smaller margin of error because the sample estimate is based on more data.

steps.title: Compare the two samples
steps.content:
The second sample has $288$ members instead of $144$ members:
$$
288=2(144)
$$
Doubling the sample size makes the margin of error lower. It does not guarantee that the estimated percentage itself will be higher or lower.

steps.title: Choose the likely effect
steps.content: The most likely effect is that the margin of error will be lower. The answer is A.
